findings.evidence: `FunctorOfPoints.lean:11-14` says `Spec H` and `H` not commutative; `FunctorOfPoints.lean:68,126` assume only `[Semiring H]`; roadmap grep: `ReductiveGroups/README.md:21` defines affine group schemes by commutative Hopf algebras and `:99` says the functor lives on commutative value algebras.
findings.fix: Either restrict the group-scheme-facing statements/docs to `[CommSemiring H]`, or rephrase the general declarations as convolution groups of algebra homs from an arbitrary Hopf algebra and add a separate commutative-Hopf specialization for functor-of-points language.
findings.issue: The file says `Spec H` is an affine group scheme even though the declarations only assume `[Semiring H]` and explicitly say `H` need not be commutative; `Spec`/affine group schemes in the roadmap use commutative Hopf algebras.

summary: The convolution group construction itself checks out, but the public framing misidentifies the noncommutative generality as an affine group scheme functor of points.
